evidence_refs.quote: We investigate whether docstring-backed property testing can be integrated into a real third-party Python repository as a locally runnable quality gate, moving beyond prior toy-level demonstrations. We implement a two-layer gate—doctest example execution and docstring-anchored Hypothesis property checking—and integrate it into `more-itertools/more-itertools` as a pytest hook. The gate executes 710 docstring examples (zero failures) and 9 docstring-anchored Hypothesis properties (1,800 generated

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Static inspection of the repository revealed: | Module | Public APIs | With docstrings | With doctest examples | |---|---|---|---| | `more_itertools/more.py` | 145 | 120 | 113 | | `more_itertools/recipes.py` | 56 | 53 | 50 | The high ratio of doctest-bearing docstrings made this repository a favorable candidate for a docstring-backed gate.

claims.claim: We implemented `scripts/docstring_property_gate.py` with two layers: **Layer 1: Docstring example gate.** This layer runs `doctest.testmod` on `more_itertools.recipes` and `more_itertools.more` with `ELLIPSIS` and `NORMALIZE_WHITESPACE` option flags.
